A classical sculpture of this figure is theorized to be the same artwork that received the highest praise in the writings of Pliny the Elder, and was unearthed near the Oppian hill. A film by this artist was originally scored by Paul Hindemith, but that soundtrack is now lost due to the destruction of the work under the Nazi “degenerate art” campaign. parts: Name this exhibition hall, financed by Karl Wittgenstein. Its exterior features a large blue sphere that is decorated with gold-colored leaves, and it is featured on the €0.50 Austrian coin.
The Secession Building houses this work, which covers the walls of its lower B. chamber. Its central piece features Typhoeus the Giant, along with the Gorgon sisters.
Gustav Klimt, who created the Beethoven Frieze, completed this “last masterpiece” of his the year he died. It is set to be auctioned by Sotheby’s in the summer of 2023.

652ebb788051389c667bd25a.leadin: Answer some questions about the work of Zaha Hadid, for 10 points each:
652ebb788051389c667bd25a.leadin_sanitized: Answer some questions about the work of Zaha Hadid, for 10 points each:
parts: This neo-futurist center, named after a former president of the Republic of Azerbaijan, displays Hadid’s characteristic flowing style. It is featured on the 200 manat banknote. It held a personal exhibition for Tahir Salahov, titled “At the turn of the century.”
Hadid utilized the concept of the “Metonymic Landscape” in her “Design Center” located in this historic district of Seoul. This district is named for its cardinal direction as a former gate of Seoul.
Hadid used dramatic curved arches in a bridge named for this man. This former C. leader of the UAE was the dedicatee of an installation titled The Constellation in Abu Dhabi.
parts_sanitized: This neo-futurist center, named after a former president of the Republic of Azerbaijan, displays Hadid's characteristic flowing style. It is featured on the 200 manat banknote. It held a personal exhibition for Tahir Salahov, titled "At the turn of the century."

Hadid used dramatic curved arches in a bridge named for this man. This former C. leader of the UAE was the dedicatee of an installation titled The Constellation in Abu Dhabi.
answers: Dongdaemun [accept Heunginjimun, I guess, even though the design center is named for Dongdaemun]
answers_sanitized: Dongdaemun [accept Heunginjimun, I guess, even though the design center is named for Dongdaemun]

652ebb788051389c667bd25d.leadin: Not all architectural designs come to fruition. For 10 points each:
652ebb788051389c667bd25d.leadin_sanitized: Not all architectural designs come to fruition. For 10 points each:
parts: This architect created many fantastical and atmospheric plans for prisons, which are included in his etching series “Imaginary Prisons.”
This other architect included animal heads on spikes in his plan for the gates of a hunting ground. He included the likeness of a cow for his depiction of a barn.
Because many of Piranesi’s and Lequeu’s works are not meant to be constructed in real life, they fall under this term. Architecture under this term is also known as visionary architecture.

652ebb788051389c667bd261.leadin: The first gathering of this group took advantage of a power outage, where they aimed to make rebellious and humorous artwork with only candlelight. For 10 points each:
652ebb788051389c667bd261.leadin_sanitized: The first gathering of this group took advantage of a power outage, where they aimed to make rebellious and humorous artwork with only candlelight. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Parisian, proto-Dadaist art movement founded by Jules Levy.
This caricaturist and “father of the animated cartoon” was a member of the Incoherents. His later works include animated films like Fantasmagorie.

Eugene Bataille created a depiction of this figure smoking a pipe for an 1883 exhibition with the Incoherents. Bataille’s work predates a similar appropriation of this figure by Marcel Duchamp by 26 years.

652ebb788051389c667bd268.leadin: She’s not Frida Kahlo, but this artist suffered an accident at a young age, which forced her to be bedridden for a significant part of her childhood. For 10 points each:
652ebb788051389c667bd268.leadin_sanitized: She's not Frida Kahlo, but this artist suffered an accident at a young age, which forced her to be bedridden for a significant part of her childhood. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this surrealist poet and artist who worked in Mexico for a large part of her life. In the 1940s, she wrote the script and designed the production Orion, el gran hombre del cielo.
Rahon pioneered the usage of this artistic technique on canvas. This technique conventionally consists of scratching designs onto pottery in order to reveal base layers of glaze.

Rahon was a contributor to this art magazine, which was founded by her C. then-husband, Wolfgang Paalen. Only six issues of this magazine were ever produced, and it frequently featured insights into Totem art and other indigenous-inspired mediums.
